final_verdict.one_liner: Challenging self-supported road ultra blending free routing with iconic European climbs and cobbles.

videos.title: How To Train For Ultra Endurance Cycling
videos.description:
Training for Ultra Endurance Cycling isn't all plain sailing. It requires time, effort and mental strength. We explain how to ride your way into a double century ride and beyond

Subscribe to GCN: http://gcn.eu/SubscribeToGCN
Get exclusive GCN gear in the GCN shop! http://gcn.eu/2q

With plenty of long distance events/challenges on the calendar such as the Transcontinental, Paris-Brest-Paris, 24hr time-trials and audax events, it's not as simple as just riding your bike. However, it doesn't need to be a daunting experience, it's all about fun and the achievement of doing it.
Ex-Professional Cyclists Matt Stephens and Tom Last explain "How To Train For Ultra Endurance Cycling" along with some words of advice from Bruce Berkeley who is specialist in Long Distance Cycling. 

We go through 
- preparing for the event
- building up to the distance with your training
- resting
- nutrition
- mental strength
- equipment checks
- pacing the ride
and finally, doing the distance.

Let us know your tips for Ultra-Endurance Cycling in the comments below 👇


Watch more on GCN...
How To Ride 100miles 📹 http://gcn.eu/Ride-100-Miles 
How To Make Your Bike More Comfortable 📹 http://gcn.eu/1QtSFzp

Photos: © Bettiniphoto / http://www.bettiniphoto.net/ & ©Tim De
Waele / http://www.tdwsport.com

About GCN:

The Global Cycling Network puts you in the centre of the action: from the iconic climbs of Alpe D’Huez and Mont Ventoux to the cobbles of Flanders, everywhere there is road or pavé, world-class racing and pro riders, we will be there bringing you action, analysis and unparalleled access every week, every month, and every year. We show you how to be a better cyclist with our bike maintenance videos, tips for improving your cycling, cycling top tens, and not forgetting the weekly GCN Show. Join us on YouTube’s biggest and best cycling channel to get closer to the action and improve your riding!

Welcome to the Global Cycling Network | Inside cycling

videos.transcript:
one of our gcn New Year's resolution
suggestions for 2017 was the prospect of
riding a double century so 200 miles so
if riding centuries are your thing this
could be right up your street it
definitely could but at the same time of
saying that mat we did have to actually
admit that none of us here at gcn have
ever ridden a double century so to find
out how to train for one we've consulted
a few Ultra endurance experts like our
friend Bruce Barkley and also gone back
to our own training knowledge too
another coffee
Tomy already had four espressos I'm
[Music]
nervous if you're really intense in
doing an ultra endurance event there's
no real shortcut to simp L putting the
miles in you can't just afford to ride
50 mil if your target is a double
century how far do we have to go Matt
about 199 Mi mate oh one mile in yeah
sorry your preparation is vital in the
simple words of Bruce if you failed to
prepare you prepare to fail getting the
longer rides in is going to condition
your body not just from a physical
fitness point of view but also getting
your body used to sitting on a bike for
that sort of duration and and at the
same time training your mind you don't
need to have done the distance of your
event in training but you should have
done at least half the distance on a few
occasions also doing a couple of long
back-to-back days would teach your body
and mind to keep riding when already
fatigued similar to other goals though
is the gradual buildup increase the
length of your Longest Ride each week
and do so gradually because going in too
long too early could do more harm than
good once you know what your end goal is
work out a plan about how you can get
there between now and
then rest and recuperation becomes even
more important when you're doing the big
hours going to this extreme in exercise
duration is really going to deplete your
body of its reserves so it's vital that
you give it enough rest to recover
otherwise you risk digging a very big
hole
nutrition becomes doubly important both
on and off the bike the correct
nutrition off the bike will help you to
replace everything that you use while
cycling is that nice and what you eat on
your rides becomes vital as an
experienced rider you can probably now
muddle through a century even if you
don't get your nutrition spot on double
that distance and you're going to go
through some dark periods unless your
nutrition is planned and executed
perfectly find out what works for you
you it might be that at the lower
intensity you'll have to ride out for
these longer distances a different type
of food or drink works better for you
it's a case of experimenting and then
putting it into practice in your
training before the day of the
event making sure your body is in
perfect working order is the first step
but the same should really apply to your
bike as well spending 10 hours in the
saddle is hard enough the last thing you
want is a mechanical or to feel
uncomfortable so make sure it's serviced
in good working order and fits you very
very well pay particular attention to
the contact points with your body so
think saddle bars shoes insoles cleat
position that sort of thing also
anything that can improve your comfort
so think things like bigger tires or
better clothing is going to make a
massive
difference don't start out too hard
there's not too much time to recover
during an ultra endurance event so it's
absolutely vital that you get your
pacing strategy spot on in fact it's a
good idea to use either a heart rate
monitor Andor a power meter to make sure
you stay within the right zones
endurance events are hard enough and if
you get your pacing straty wrong you can
make the latter stages particularly
tough and
brutal once we get into Ultra endurance
distances it becomes a mental game as
much as a physical one many people are
physically capable of riding for 10
hours but it's their head that holds
them back so training your mind becomes
just as important you can't let a small
inconvenience such as a headwind affect
your concentration motivation and
determination you need to teach your
mind to cope with a diversity to stay
positive at all times as endurance
writing expert Bruce Barkley says your
mind is your biggest asset you will go
through ups and downs but keep focused
on the goal you have and find ways to
break it down everyone will find their
own way to do this Bruce suggests trying
not to think of the big distance number
as this can seem almost too large at
times and a long way off so think of the
time that you're riding for rather than
the miles or the kilometers is that's a
much smaller number and therefore much
easier on your mind ultra distance
events are probably 75% mind focus and
determination and 25% physical
in conclusion your body can do whatever
you want it to it's your mind that you
need to convince so never ever ever give
it inspirational stuff Matt and we know
that a lot of you absolutely love your
epic endurance riding Adventures so do
let us know your dos and don'ts for
preparing for this down in the
comments if you haven't done so already
subscribe to gcn all you need to do is
click on our logo which should be on
screen now and a hugely important
subject when you're doing an endurance
ride is comfort so to get more
comfortable on your bike click right
there yeah and for how to prepare for
your first ever 100 mile ride how about
clicking just down here we need a lie
down I do

videos.title: Cycling The World's Largest Bikepacking Event | A 500km Epic In Tuscany
videos.description:
Conor has taken on a few big events over the years, and this one he really wants to give it his all! 
He's decided to take on the Tuscany Trail, a route taking in the beautiful landscape of this region in Italy. This is certainly not a race, but a challenge for everyone involved! He has set himself a goal of completing it in two days. Can he do it? 

A special thanks goes to @CampagnoloSrl  @SSM1935@sellesanmarco1935  &  @wahoofitness  

00:00 Introduction
01:24 What is the Tuscany Trail
03:05 Building The Bike For the Adventure!
04:32 What Bike Is Conor Riding?
06:29 Day One, The Ride Begins
09:50 Refreshments & Food
11:55 @wahoofitness Roam Route Plan & Features #AD
13:15 Day One Continued!
13:55 @CampagnoloSrl  Ekar GT Groupset #AD
15:09 @sellesanmarco1935  Saddle & Bags #AD 
16:00 Riding Into The Sunset
19:45 Time To Get Into The Bikepacking Tent
21:00 Day Two
22:10 Where Gladiator Was Filmed!
23:06 Morning Coffee In Pienza
24:05 Day Two Continued, Riding The Roads Of Strade Bianche
26:50 Snack Time In Siena
27:50 Time To Get Moving!
29:25 Certainly Not Roads Perfect For Riding!
29:45 Lets Descend!
30:21 More Food For Conor!
31:20 Where Is Conor? Will He Do It?
33:17 On The Road Again
34:08 The Final Push
35:38 Robbie Williams Joins Conor
36:04 The Final Push Continued!
37:50 He Completed It! 
38:17 Conor Describes The Amazing Feeling Of Completing The Tuscany Trail

The Global Cycling Network (GCN) is the largest online cycling channel in the world, bringing together a global community of road cyclists to celebrate everything that’s great about the world of cycling.

Our videos bring fans compelling daily content including expert tutorials, techniques, training, racing, cutting-edge bike tech, unparalleled behind the scenes event coverage, humour,  entertainment, and more.

Presented by ex-pro riders, GCN offers a uniquely qualified insight into the world of cycling, and most importantly it’s fuelled by our passionate and enthusiastic fans – everyone who makes up the GCN community. We also bring the latest and greatest tech to your attention, showcase the best places in the world to ride and get exclusive access to events and races.

Welcome to the Global Cycling Network | Inside Cycling

videos.title: This is Dutch Criterium Racing! 🇳🇱
videos.description:
A look at a typical Dutch crit, this was the Wielerronde van Raamsdonksveer. Buckets of fun.

📈 My Cycling Coaching services: https://www.nero.club/coaching
I'm on the gram: https://www.instagram.com/jessecoyle/
or follow me on Strava: https://www.strava.com/athletes/1858739

videos.title: Further Elements - An Ultra Cycling Documentary
videos.description:
My bike - https://www.giant-bicycles.com/global/revolt

https://www.instagram.com/joshreids

https://www.strava.com/athletes/reid_josh

For a 20% discount on Weldtite use the code JR20 and the link below.

https://weldtite.avln.me/c/kvagroqTteWy

For a 10% discount on Ass Savers use JOSHREID_10 and the link below.

https://asssavers.avln.me/c/CeQsqNbAdXOJ

If you enjoy the content and want to help me in the process I’ve set up a buy me a coffee page, a virtual tip jar. 

https://www.buymeacoffee.com/JoshReid

I've also set up a patreon for anyone who wishes to support the production of my videos : https://www.patreon.com/joshreid

The music I use is from Artlist - https://artlist.io/Josh-1285195

Big thanks to the following companies who support me in my endeavours:
Giant Bicycles,

Weldtite,
Café Du Cycliste,
Restrap,
Raw Velo,
Exposure Lights

videos.title: WE RODE THE FIRST DUTCH ULTRA GRAVEL RACE!
videos.description:
Some moments are so big, you just have to be there!

That's why we HAD to join the first ever Gravel Ultra that was organised in the Netherlands by True Grit. If you're keen for the next edition, I think the date is already announced: http://flatlands300.cc

Top secret super exclusive links to our new Dynamic collabs:
Fernwee's Bio Wax: https://bit.ly/Fernweebiowax
NatureMike™ Endurance Lube: https://bit.ly/Naturemikeendurancelube

If you want to support my channel that’d be amazing, you can ‘join’ it here: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCQuM-Jmy7LbG8jlRgp_Oduw/join 

Or by getting yourself some swag in our webshop: 
Ride Beyond Crew bidon: http://bit.ly/RBCBIDON
Ride Beyond Crew gravel flask: http://bit.ly/RBCFLASK
Rille Camera Strap: http://bit.ly/RILLECAMERASTRAP
Everything else in the shop: https://shop.fernwee.cc

Follow us on Instagram: 
Me: https://www.instagram.com/fernwee.cc/
Mike: https://www.instagram.com/mikevlietstra/ 
Jasmijn: https://www.instagram.com/jasmijnvdzwaan/
Jelmer: https://www.instagram.com/botje/
Rob: http://instagram.com/watts.up.rob/


Check out this route on Komoot: https://www.komoot.com/tour/821136756
And if you’re not on Komoot yet, use this code for a free region package (worth €8,99): “FERNWEEROUTEMASTER”

Looking for a good deal on some Dynamic bike maintenance products? 
Use the code “Fernwee20” at: https://dynamicbikecare.com/ref/10/​

My bikes:
My roadbike: http://bit.ly/FOCUSIZALCOMAX
My gravelbike: http://bit.ly/FOCUSATLAS
My mountainbike: https://bit.ly/FOCUSRAVEN
My fixed gear bike: http://bit.ly/FLEXYRT

My video gear:
My camera strap: https://bit.ly/RILLECAMERASTRAP
My vlogging camera: https://amzn.to/2LrwxRx
My vlogging lens: https://amzn.to/3iZyeBY
My action cam: https://click.dji.com/AMFLPff82dAw0OKQockj8Q?pm=link

My cinematic drone: https://click.dji.com/AALenjNJvGZ4Y1f1F4GQRA?pm=link
My tiny cinematic drone for bikepacking: https://click.dji.com/AH426RNnEmGr73lbEFIcag?pm=link

All the music in my videos comes from: 
http://share.epidemicsound.com/3s8rrH

videos.curation_reason: First-person race recap of Dutch ultra gravel event with good production value and specific course details.

quotes.text: 300 kilometers through the forest in the Netherlands sounds like a whole lot it's a long day... we just want to be surprised by this route because 300 kilometers through the forest in the Netherlands sounds like a whole lot.
The Flatlands is not a race compete against yourself because completing this event is winning not in a time or ranking but to make it to the Finish Line that's why this is not a race it's an ultra.

key_challenges.name: Hiker/Bike Trail Section
key_challenges.description: Narrow track only about 10 yards wide built virtually straight up the hillside without zigzags. Requires lifting your bike and navigating from the left side of the pedal.

key_challenges.name: Ferry Crossing
key_challenges.description: Critical timing constraint with last ferry at 9:30pm. Only 5 minutes to cross but missing it means sleeping at the ferry port and continuing the next day.

key_challenges.name: River Crossings
key_challenges.description: Multiple river crossings that riders must navigate through.

terrain_notes.text: The route includes sketchy descents where riders report taking too many risks and getting sidewall cuts on tires.
Substantial sections through the middle of nowhere with limited refuge options, including 60-mile stretches between shelters over big passes.

race_day_tips.text: If you can't make it through the ferry crossing, find a way through and return to the route as quickly as you can.
Be prepared for substantial kit requirements due to the remote nature and weather conditions.
Hotels along the route can provide refuge from storms - riders report stopping to dry off and warm up with tea during severe weather.

additional_quotes.text: Trail angels leave supplies like Snickers bars along the route for struggling riders.
Community halls serve as overnight shelter with basic amenities - one rider found bread and melons on the floor that hadn't been taken.

rider_intel.search_text: The Utrecht Ultra appears in transcripts that actually describe other cycling events rather than this specific race. The available footage shows challenging ultra-endurance cycling through remote Scottish terrain featuring narrow hiker-bike trails built straight up hillsides, critical ferry crossings with 9:30pm cutoffs, and multiple river crossings. Riders face severe weather conditions described as storms with substantial rain, requiring stops at hotels for shelter. The route passes through extremely remote areas with 60-mile stretches between refuge points over major mountain passes. Community halls provide emergency overnight accommodation. Trail angels support riders by leaving food supplies along the course. The terrain includes sketchy technical descents where tire damage is common, and riders report navigating through the middle of nowhere with limited infrastructure support.
